# Transcriber — local setup instructions for AI agents

Follow these steps in order to set up and run Transcriber locally.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

Check if `git` is installed:

```
git --version
```

If missing, install it from https://git-scm.com/downloads.

Check if `pixi` is installed:

```
pixi --version
```

If missing, install it:

- macOS / Linux: `curl -fsSL https://pixi.sh/install.sh | sh`
- Windows: `winget install prefix-dev.pixi`

Restart the shell after installing pixi so the binary is on PATH.

---

## 2. Clone the repository

```
git clone https://github.com/vasiliadi/transcriber.git
cd transcriber
```

---

## 3. Configure secrets

The app reads credentials from `.streamlit/secrets.toml`. Create that file now:

```
mkdir -p .streamlit
```

Then create `.streamlit/secrets.toml` with the following content, filling in real values:

```toml
GEMINI_API_KEY = "..."
REPLICATE_API_TOKEN = "..."
HF_ACCESS_TOKEN = "..."
# PROXY = "..."  # optional — remove the leading # and set a value only if needed
```

**Prompt the user for each value.** Do not generate, guess, or skip any key. If the user does not have a key yet, direct them to the relevant page:

- `GEMINI_API_KEY` — https://ai.google.dev/
- `REPLICATE_API_TOKEN` — https://replicate.com/account/api-tokens
- `HF_ACCESS_TOKEN` — https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens
  - After obtaining the token, the user must also accept the model conditions at:
    - https://hf.co/pyannote/segmentation-3.0
    - https://hf.co/pyannote/speaker-diarization-3.1

Do **not** write API key values into any issue, log, or output.

---

## 4. Run the app

```
pixi run start
```

This installs all dependencies (including Python and ffmpeg) on first run, then starts the Streamlit server. When you see output like `You can now view your Streamlit app in your browser`, open:

```
http://localhost:8501
```

---

## 5. Troubleshooting

If any step fails, open an issue at:

https://github.com/vasiliadi/transcriber/issues/new

Include the following details in the issue — **never include API key values**:

- OS and architecture (e.g. output of `uname -a` on macOS/Linux, or `ver` on Windows)
- Pixi version (`pixi --version`)
- Which step failed (clone / secrets / `pixi run start`)
- Last ~30 lines of terminal output from the failing command
- Confirmation that `.streamlit/secrets.toml` exists and contains all three required keys (names only, not values)
